title: Judge Me, God of My Salvation

verses.text: Judge me, God of my salvation, plead my cause, for thee I trust; hear my earnest supplication, save me from my foes unjust. O my soul, why art thou grieving? What disquiets and dismays? Hope in God; his help receiving, I shall yet my Savior praise.

On thy strength alone relying, why am I cast off by thee, in my helpless sorrow sighing, while the foe oppresses me? O my soul, why art thou grieving? What disquiets and dismays? Hope in God; his help receiving, I shall yet my Savior praise.

Light and truth, my way attending, send thou forth to be my guide, till thy holy mount ascending, I within thy house abide. O my soul, why art thou grieving? What disquiets and dismays? Hope in God; his help receiving, I shall yet my Savior praise.

At thy sacred altar bending, God, my God, my boundless joy, harp and voice, in worship blending, for thy praise will I employ. O my soul, why art thou grieving? What disquiets and dismays? Hope in God; his help receiving, I shall yet my Savior praise.
